name: 10-stop-sequence
description: Canonical scenario #10: stop_sequence terminal path. Anthropic-side scripted response sets stop_reason='stop_sequence' with stopSequence='###'; OR-side /responses scripted response sets status='completed' with the stop sequence baked into the assistant text (the /responses agent loop has no separate stop-sequence concept — the model's text contains the sequence and the loop terminates normally). The parity claim asserted: both SDKs reach terminal:success and the final assistant text contains the canon stop sequence verbatim (asserted via the comparator's positional event-stream check). Per ambiguity call #5 from issue 127, observed behavior: the Anthropic SDK does NOT strip the matched stop sequence from the assistant text on its way to the host; both sides surface '###' in the final text. If a future SDK update starts stripping, this scenario's final-text comparison would break and the failure would document the change.
prompt: List the first 5 prime numbers, one per line. End with ###.
systemPrompt: You are a helpful assistant. Use the requested terminator.
